name: explicit_conventional_repo
goal: Keep Conventional Commits when explicit repo docs require them.

name: explicit_gitmoji_allowlist_repo
goal: Enable gitmoji strongly and reject emojis outside the repo-local allowlist.

name: plain_imperative_history_repo
goal: Preserve plain imperative subjects when history is clearly plain.

name: mixed_history_without_rules
goal: Fall back to Conventional Commits when history is mixed and explicit rules are absent.

name: presentational_modal_cleanup
goal: Do not overclassify UI layout, spacing, or wrapper cleanup as bugfix.

name: actual_validation_bugfix
goal: Classify actual broken behavior or validation failures as bugfix.

name: file_move_or_legacy_move
goal: Classify file moves as move or structure, not generic bugfix.

name: title_only_preferred_repo
goal: Allow safe title-only drafting when history does not require commit bodies.

name: global_commit_template_ignored
goal: Ignore global commit.template files outside the repository when deciding repo-local explicit rules.

name: multiline_body_without_literal_backslash_n
goal: Create multiline commit bodies without storing a literal \n sequence.

name: korean_emoji_repo_phrasing
goal: In a Korean-centric emoji repo, polish awkward translated wording into shorter and more natural Korean commit wording.

name: korean_verbose_summary_polish
goal: Shorten verbose Korean phrasing like '...을 위한' or '...에 대한' into natural commit wording without changing meaning.

name: mixed_repo_language_profile
goal: Infer dominant language and tone from mixed history instead of forcing one global phrasing style.

name: explicit_conventional_repo_with_natural_wording
goal: Keep explicit conventional format while still polishing the summary wording naturally.

name: explicit_gitmoji_repo_with_natural_wording
goal: Keep the allowlist and gitmoji mode while polishing the summary wording to match repo-local phrasing.

name: commit_helper_invocation_boundary
goal: When commit-helper is invoked, do not add external orchestration, Lore, or OmX co-author metadata unless repo-local rules or the user explicitly require it.
